hook: Resale landed is still trading — but the discount gap is widening 📉

slides.title: Landed: New vs Resale
slides.subtitle: Who is selling, where they're selling, and what they're giving up to close.

slides.headline: All landed deals in this dataset are resale (typeOfSale=3)
slides.footnote: URA REALIS — every transaction in the current landed pull is a sub-sale or resale. Zero new developer launches recorded this week.

slides.headline: Sentosa Cove Coral Island detached hit $22.8M in Sep 2022 — latest Feb 2025 deal: $14.8M
slides.footnote: URA REALIS — Coral Island, D04, 99-yr lease. Peak $22.8M (Sep 2022) vs $14.8M (Feb 2025). Same ~737 sqm land.

slides.title: Where Landed Money Is Moving

items.label: D04 Sentosa Cove CCR
items.sub: Detached, 99-yr, Coral Island. Down 35% from $22.8M 2022 peak. Lease decay biting.

items.label: D05 Faber Hills OCR
items.sub: Semi-D peak Nov 2024. Most active landed cluster — 30+ deals since 2021, freehold.

items.label: D05 Springwood RCR
items.sub: Freehold terrace, Mar 2026 deal. Steady climb from $3.2M in 2022 — +74% in 4 yrs.

items.label: D05 Zehnder Rd RCR
items.sub: Semi-D, freehold. Sep 2021 deal. Thin liquidity — only 2 transactions on record.

slides.footnote: URA REALIS resale transactions. All land-area basis. Faber Hills & Springwood show strongest recent volume.

slides.title: 3 Signals From the Resale Data
items: Faber Hills semi-Ds (D05 OCR, freehold) peaked at $8.39M in Dec 2024 — the Jan 2025 deal at $7.1M suggests early softening at the top of the range.
Springwood terraces (D05 RCR, freehold) are the quiet winner: $3.2M in May 2022 → $5.58M in Mar 2026, a 74% run on small 171–258 sqm plots.
Sentosa Cove (D04, 99-yr) is the clearest cautionary tale: every 2024–2025 deal has printed below the 2022–2023 highs, with Feb 2025 at $14.8M vs $22.8M peak — a $8M haircut.
The Vision strata terraces (D05, 99-yr) are flat: $3.35M in Nov 2021, $3.38M in Sep 2025 — four years of zero capital growth on leasehold strata.
No new launch supply in this week's data — 100% resale. Buyers have zero new-build optionality in the landed segment right now.

slides.title: Semi-D Price Arc 2021 → 2025

items.label: Peak: Nov 2024
items.sub: Detached, 419 sqm land. Highest recorded price in the estate.

items.label: Mid: Apr 2024
items.sub: Semi-D, 372 sqm. Strong mid-year 2024 print before softening.

items.label: Latest: Jan 2025
items.sub: Semi-D, 372 sqm — same size as Apr 2024 deal, $500K cheaper. Watch this.

items.label: Base: Oct 2021
items.sub: Semi-D, 544 sqm. Entry-level 2021 pricing — shows how far the estate has run.

slides.footnote: URA REALIS — Faber Hills, Faber Avenue, D05 OCR freehold. Land-area transactions only.

slides.title: Where the Real Value Sits Right Now
slides.body: Freehold terraces in D05 RCR (Springwood, Jalan Mat Jambol) are the standout value play — freehold tenure, RCR location, still trading below $6M, and 74% appreciation since 2022 with room left. Sentosa Cove looks cheap vs peak but don't be fooled: leasehold decay accelerates from here and foreigners still face ABSD. Faber Hills semi-Ds are plateauing — the Jan 2025 deal at $7.1M vs Nov 2024's $8.39M is a 15% reset in two months.
slides.quote: Buy freehold RCR landed under $6M before the next rate cut — that's the window right now.

caption:
Resale landed is still closing — but not all deals are equal 📉

• Sentosa Cove detached: $22.8M peak (2022) → $14.8M in Feb 2025. That's a $8M haircut.
• Faber Hills semi-Ds plateauing: Jan 2025 at $7.1M vs $8.39M just 2 months earlier.
• Springwood freehold terraces quietly up 74% since 2022 — still under $6M.

Freehold RCR landed under $6M is the only segment with both tenure safety and upside left.
